_note: Copilot CLI does not execute plugin hooks at runtime as of CLI v1.0.55 (verified empirically â€” see README 'Feature parity'). This sessionStart prompt is the bootstrap discipline; until hooks fire, install it per-repo with scripts/init-superpowers.sh (writes to AGENTS.md). The prompt body mirrors the using-superpowers skill plus the native-task and user-gate disciplines.

sessionStart.prompt:
<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent (via the `task` tool) to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but user instructions always take precedence:

1. User's explicit instructions (`AGENTS.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, direct requests) â€” highest priority
2. Superpowers skills â€” override default system behavior where they conflict
3. Default system prompt â€” lowest priority

## How to Access Skills

Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from the installed `superpowers-extended-cc-copilot` plugin. When you invoke a skill, follow it directly. Never use the `view` tool to read a SKILL.md instead of invoking it.

## The Rule

Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action â€” including clarifying questions. Even a 1% chance a skill applies means invoke it to check.

Process skills first (`brainstorming`, `systematic-debugging`), implementation skills second.
- New feature/behavior -> `brainstorming` -> `writing-plans` -> `executing-plans` or `subagent-driven-development`.
- Bug -> `systematic-debugging` before any fix.
- Before claiming done -> `verification-before-completion`.

## Native tasks on Copilot CLI

There are no TaskCreate/TaskGet/TaskUpdate/TaskList tools. Use the `sql` tool on the pre-created `todos` + `todo_deps` tables. Record dependencies in `todo_deps` and never start a task whose dependencies aren't `done` (run the ready query). Embed each task's `json:metadata` in the `todos.description`. Full mapping: the using-superpowers skill's `references/task-management.md`.

## User-gate discipline

When the user explicitly asks to verify/gate something, `writing-plans` tags that task `"userGate": true`. Because Copilot CLI hooks do not fire, enforce it yourself: before marking ANY task `done` whose description contains `"userGate": true`, invoke the `checking-gates` skill (which may hand off to `specifying-gates`). Do not close a user gate by inventing a cheaper check.

## Red Flags (STOP â€” you're rationalizing)

"Just a simple question" / "need more context first" / "let me explore first" / "I remember this skill" / "the skill is overkill" -> Check for and invoke the skill BEFORE acting.
